description_long: This object is a compact, handheld two-way radio or walkie-talkie, featuring a long, slender antenna extending from the top. The front face displays a small screen or speaker grille above a star-shaped logo, with two rotary knobs and a button on the top for power and channel selection. The sides are ribbed for grip and have a series of raised, tactile buttons for easy operation, likely for volume and push-to-talk functions. The back is smooth and slightly curved, with no visible clip or attachment. The casing appears to be made of durable black plastic, designed for rugged use in urban environments. The overall design is ergonomic, fitting comfortably in one hand and suitable for portable communication.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, ribbed sides, and a star logo on the front. It features a small screen or speaker, rotary knobs, and tactile side buttons.
description_view_0: Side view showing the ribbed grip and a row of raised buttons along the side.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the small screen or speaker, star logo, and top-mounted knobs and antenna.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with ribbed grip and side buttons, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a smooth, curved back with no visible features except the antenna and top controls.
